source.sourcePath: raw/sources/Project/02_CYAN/4. 시험소 제출자료(601-1)/4. Risk management/A-RMR-CYN_Risk Management Report (Rev.0 250525).pdf

document.title: Risk Management Report for GT Series Digital Flat Panel X-ray Detector

sourceSummary.oneLine: H&abyz Co., Ltd. risk management report for GT1717C and GT1717CP digital flat panel X-ray detectors.
sourceSummary.officialUse: Documents risk management activities, risk evaluation, risk control, residual risk acceptability, and production/post-production information for the device.
keyPoints: The device is a wired digital flat panel detector for general radiographic diagnosis of human anatomy in adults and children.
Applicable national and regional regulations listed are Regulation (EU) 2017/745 and the Medical Device Act in Korea.
Risk acceptability criteria define acceptable risk levels as 1 to 4 and unacceptable risk levels as 5 to 25.
After risk control measures, all identified residual risks are reported as distributed in an acceptable region.
Production and post-production information collection includes customer surveys, complaints, inspection monitoring, standards monitoring, market information, and adverse event reporting if necessary.

concepts.name: Risk acceptability criteria
concepts.evidence: Acceptable Risk (A): Acceptable Region, Green Zone, Risk levels: 1~4 Unacceptable Risk (U): Unacceptable Region, Red Zone, Risk levels: 5~25

concepts.name: Production and post-production information
concepts.evidence: activities related to collection and review of relevant production and post-production information

concepts.name: Intended purpose
concepts.evidence: The GT1717C/GT1717CP is indicated for digital imaging solution designed for providing general radiographic diagnosis of human anatomy targeting both adult and children.

evidence.claim: The document is Risk Management Report A-RMR-CYN Rev.0 dated May 25, 2025.
evidence.evidenceText: Technical Documentation Document No. A-RMR-CYN Rev. No. 0 Risk Management Report Rev. Date May 25, 2025

evidence.claim: The report applies to GT1717C and GT1717CP digital flat panel X-ray detector models.
evidence.evidenceText: Digital Flat Panel X-ray Detector Model name: GT1717C, GT1717CP

evidence.claim: The document lists Regulation (EU) 2017/745 and the Medical Device Act in Korea as applicable national and regional regulations.
evidence.evidenceText: European Union Regulation (EU) 2017/745 of the European Parliament and of the Council of 5 April 2017; Republic of Korea Medical Device Act in Korea

evidence.claim: The intended use is digital imaging for general radiographic diagnosis of human anatomy targeting adults and children.
evidence.evidenceText: Digital Flat Panel X-ray Detector (Ref no. GT1717C, GT1717CP) is indicated for digital imaging solution designed for providing general radiographic diagnosis of human anatomy targeting both adult and children.

evidence.claim: The patient population is adults and children except infants and pregnant women.
evidence.evidenceText: Patient population Adult and children except infants and pregnant women.

evidence.claim: The device is not to be used for mammography, infants, pregnant women, fluoroscopy, or angiography.
evidence.evidenceText: Not to be used for mammography. Not to be used for infant and pregnant women. Not to be used for fluoroscopy and angiography.

evidence.claim: The device expected service life is stated as 5 years in the general details section.
evidence.evidenceText: Product Name Digital Flat Panel X-ray Detector Model Name GT1717C, GT1717CP Expected service life 5 years

evidence.claim: The device uses TFT and CsI scintillator technology and supports auto exposure detection without generator integration.
evidence.evidenceText: The detector consists of TFT and Scintillator (CsI) and an electronic device that converts data read through it into digital signals. The detectors support an auto exposure detection technology that allows the detectors to be used without generator integration.
evidence.pageTarget: Digital flat panel X-ray detector technology

evidence.claim: Risk analysis includes intended use and reasonably foreseeable misuse, safety characteristics, hazards and hazardous situations, and risk estimation.
evidence.evidenceText: The risk analysis process consists of the following steps... description of the intended use... identification of the characteristics... identification of hazards and hazardous situations... estimation of risks for each hazardous situation

evidence.claim: Before risk control, the document reports 2 acceptable risks and 47 unacceptable risks.
After risk control, the document reports 49 acceptable risks and 0 unacceptable risks.
evidence.evidenceText: Risk evaluation results after risk control... EN ISO 14971:2019 49 0 49 Sum 49 0 49

evidence.claim: Overall residual risks are determined to be acceptable through warning, operating method, adverse event reporting, and usability expert review.
evidence.evidenceText: Finally, the overall residual risks are determined to be acceptable through the warning, operating method and adverse event reporting and usability expert review.

evidence.claim: Production and post-production review includes customer satisfaction surveys, complaints, inspection results, measuring devices, design changes, standards monitoring, market information, process management, and purchaser assessment results.
evidence.evidenceText: Quality Assurance 1. Customer satisfaction survey... Statistical processing of customer complaints... Monitoring results of the incoming inspection and end product test results... R&D 1. Design changes... Monitoring the national and international standards... Production Process management monitoring... Management Monitoring the purchaser assessment results

evidence.claim: The Quality manager is responsible for collecting and reviewing production and post-production information and reporting safety information and side effects to the regulatory agency if necessary.
evidence.evidenceText: Responsibility and the role of the Quality manager... Collect, review the information and manage the quality problems in production and post-production phase... the Quality manager shall report safety information and any side effects to the regulatory agency

reviewFlags.reason: authoritySignal is FDA in source metadata, but the source text lists European Union and Republic of Korea regulations and does not identify FDA as an applicable authority.
Conflicting service-life evidence: page 11 states expected service life is 5 years, while IEC 60601-1 clause 4.4 states the service life is approximatelyTBD.
Conflicting risk count evidence: section 6.3 says 43 risks were identified, but tables and totals report 49 risks with 2 acceptable and 47 unacceptable before risk control.
Potential OCR or source quality issues are present, including apparent misspellings and inconsistent standard references such as EN 15322-1 where the normative references list EN 15223-1.
